project.title: Non-intein splicing of RB47 -  Activation of an Endoribonuclease by Non-intein Protein Splicing
project.projectDescription: The poly A binding protein RB47 was purified from C. reinhardtii chloroplasts and was found to have endoribonuclease activity. MS analysis suggested that an internal non-conserved linker (NCL)sequence was absent from the native protein. Recombinant, full length RB47 protein, including the NCL did not have endoribonuclease activity while versions lacking the NCL were active. An in vitro system, utilizing recombinant RB47 and chloroplast lysates, recreated the processing of RB47 in that the NCL was excised and the flanking sequences were spliced together.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Protein bands were excised from SDS PAGE gels and digested with trypsin or trypsin and formic acid prior to analysis by nanoLC-ESI-MS/MS using an Orbitrap Fusion Tribrid (Thermo-Fisher Scientific) mass spectrometer equipped with a nanospray Flex ion source, and coupled with a Dionex UltiMate3000RSLCnano system
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw spectra were subjected to database searches using Proteome Discoverer 1.4 software (Thermo-Fisher Scientific) Two missed trypsin cleavage sites were allowed, the peptide precursor tolerance was set to 10 ppm and fragment ion tolerance was set to 0.6 Da.

references.referenceLine: Campbell SJ, Stern DB. Activation of an Endoribonuclease by Non-intein Protein Splicing. J Biol Chem. 2016 291(31):15911-15922
